title: Substrate path resolution unifies on the .pi-context.json pointer (resolveContextDir); config.root is not a path-resolution input
context: FGAP-079: the schema/block file-path surface diverges — schemas/blocks are WRITTEN via projectRoot(cwd) (honors config.root) but READ via resolveContextDir(cwd) (pointer only). config.json + relations.json are pinned to resolveContextDir by NECESSITY (projectRoot must read config.json to learn config.root, so config.json cannot itself move). Therefore honoring config.root for blocks/schemas while config/relations stay at the pointer would SPLIT the substrate across two dirs — incoherent. config.root coincides with the pointer in practice (adoptConception sets config.root = relative(cwd, resolveContextDir)); the divergence is latent. The 'relocatable per config / GitHub issue #3' feature (projectRoot honoring config.root) is half-wired (writes honor it, reads don't, config/relations can't) and, fully wired, would only split the substrate. DEC-0041 already made the .pi-context.json pointer the substrate-dir SoT and config.root optional; this clarifies/extends it.
decision: Substrate path resolution unifies on the .pi-context.json pointer (resolveContextDir) for read + write, blocks + schemas + config + relations. config.root is NO LONGER a path-resolution input: projectRoot(cwd) drops its config.root branch and returns resolveContextDir(cwd), auto-unifying every write-side caller with the read side. config.root is retained as optional config DATA (DEC-0041) but does not affect resolution. Substrate relocation is via the pointer (a future /context migrate flips .pi-context.json's contextDir, moving the WHOLE substrate coherently — DEC-0036 step-5), not via config.root. Additionally, a single name validator (assertSubstrateName, /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/) guards every name->path builder so a traversal name (../, /, ..) is rejected on both read and write (now reachable from the in-pi write-schema tool, FGAP-077).
consequences: projectRoot returns resolveContextDir; schema + block read==write (the FGAP-079 divergence is gone for the whole surface, not just schemas).
config.root no longer relocates anything; the half-wired issue-#3 relocation feature is retired in favor of pointer-based relocation (coherent, whole-substrate).

Behavior-preserving in practice: adoptConception sets config.root == pointer, so projectRoot already returned the pointer dir; only the latent non-default-config.root case changes (now unified instead of split).
The project-context.test 'respects config.root override' test inverts: projectRoot now IGNORES config.root.
Path-traversal is rejected at every builder (read + write); valid block/schema names are [A-Za-z0-9_-]+ (block_kind canonical_ids).
config.schema.json root description should note root is optional metadata, not a path input (cosmetic follow-up). Extends DEC-0041; relates to DEC-0015 + DEC-0036 step-5 pointer-based relocation.
